title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL startup wraps missing secret references as destination failures
summary: Oracle MySQL startup translated missing password environment variables into destination-unavailable errors.
observed: A missing configured secret reference was reported as a pool creation or destination failure.
expected: Missing secret references must remain configuration errors.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for startup secret error classification.
impact: Operators receive the wrong failure class and may troubleshoot the destination instead of the deployment configuration.
delivery_semantics: The fix occurs before connection-pool creation and does not change write behavior.
security: Accurate configuration failures avoid logging or retrying unsafe secret states.
acceptance: Missing Oracle MySQL password_env values raise ConfigurationError during startup.
Driver connection failures still map to destination-unavailable errors.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and Oracle MySQL sink startup tests.
documentation: Document that secret resolution failures are configuration errors.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the startup error classification fix is published.
